title: mirabilis glabra var. recedens
intro: Mirabilis glabra var. recedens, also known as red four o'clock or Mexican clover, is a perennial herb belonging to the Four O'Clock family (Nyctaginaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mirabilis glabra var. recedens include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
